headline: Agentic engineering and harness design moved to the center of the quarter as evaluation methodology and privacy-preserving inference broke from the pack.
body: The dominant rotation this quarter ran through agent technique, multi-provider workflows, model context, and AI-assisted development, with the shared theme that the surrounding harness now matters more than the model itself: one Google playbook framing put the model at 10% of development success and context, tools, guardrails, and verification at 90%. Building blocks reflected this, from Agentic OS loop engineering and memory management to SQLite-backed tooling assembled with Claude and GPT-4.5. Evaluation methodology shifted in parallel, with Meta-style testing harnesses described as the emerging industry standard. Among breakouts, privacy-preserving inference posted the strongest ratio, while new technique entrants clustered around the doer-judge framework, primitive-action decomposition, Softmax, feed forward networks, and neural rendering via triangle splatting.

cited_items.title: Every Level of Claude Agentic OS Explained
Google Just Dropped a Masterclass on Agentic Engineering (It's SO Good)
